# OZARK JOE'S PICKLEBALL DAILY — AI Q&A CONTEXT BUNDLE
Source: data/snapshots/latest.json + data/master/*.json
Snapshot generated: 2026-05-12T09:31:26.243Z

## GOVERNANCE
- USA Pickleball (USAP) — official US rulebook, paddle approval, amateur sanctioning.
- UPA (United Pickleball Association) — holding company for PPA Tour + MLP after 2023 merger. UPA-Athletes is the pro-side governance arm following the 2024 UPA-USAP split.
- PPA Tour — tour-event pro structure. Owns PPA rankings + most singles/doubles tournament purses.
- Major League Pickleball (MLP) — team-franchise pro structure. 20-team Premier format starting 2026; mixed-gender team matches with the DreamBreaker singles tiebreaker.
- DUPR — official exclusive skill-rating system since 2024 USAP partnership. Auto-fed by PickleballBrackets results.

Ratings vs Rankings: DUPR ratings (2.0-8.0+) measure skill ("how good?"). PPA rankings (#1, #2, ...) measure tour position over the past 52 weeks ("winning on tour?"). They are NOT interchangeable.

## GLOSSARY (selected terms)
- Dink: a soft shot landing in the opponent's non-volley zone (kitchen).
- Kitchen: the 7-foot non-volley zone on each side of the net. You cannot volley while standing in it.
- Third shot drop: soft third shot from the baseline into the opposing kitchen, used to neutralize the serve-receive advantage.
- Erne: a volley taken outside the kitchen by jumping around it.
- ATP (around the post): a shot that goes around the net post rather than over the net.
- Bert: like an Erne but the player crosses in front of their partner.
- Stacking: a positioning strategy where partners deliberately swap court sides each rally.
- DreamBreaker: MLP's mixed-gender singles tiebreaker for tied team matches.

## RULES — 2026 USAP RULEBOOK CHANGES
Edition: 2026 (effective 2026-01-01)
- §2070 Net Post Winner clarification — A ball that legally crosses the net and bounces in the receiving court, then contacts the net post, is not an automatic fault.
- §2221 Prompt line calls required — Line calls must be made promptly. Delayed 'out' calls after partner conferences are disallowed.
- §2093 Pre-match penalty authority — Officials may issue warnings and penalties during pre-match briefings and warmup, not only during play.
- §2090, 2091 Explicit ejection authority — Explicit ejection authority for assault causing injury, and for damage to venue property.
- §2242 Rally scoring formats formalized — Rally scoring formats are formally documented in the rulebook. Remains optional for many events; restricted in some championship-qualifying events.
- §2330-2334 New Adaptive Standing Division — Eligibility criteria, assistive-device rules, two-bounce allowance, and hybrid mixed-ability play introduced.
- §volley serve (4.A.5 family) 'Clearly' added to volley-serve requirements — The word 'clearly' is added to all three volley-serve requirements (below waist, paddle head below wrist, upward arc). Borderline serves are now faulted.
- §tbd Visible second ball fault — A visible second ball (e.g., peeking from a pocket) during a live rally is now an explicit fault.
- §tbd Mandatory pre-match paddle inspection — Paddles must display 'USA Pickleball Approved' marking and appear on the approved list. Pre-match inspection is mandatory.
- §tbd Legal multi-hit extension — Legal double-hit rule extended to triple-plus hits within one continuous motion.

## TOP NEWS (recent, top 5)
Each item: title (source, date) + URL + Excerpt. Cite the URL when handing it to the user.
- "The Pickleball Shot You're Not Using Enough: The Poach" (The Dink, 2026-05-11)
  https://www.thedinkpickleball.com/the-pickleball-shot-youre-not-using-enough-the-poach/
  Excerpt: The pickleball poach isn't random aggression—it's a calculated read of your opponent's patterns and positioning. Master this net-dominating shot to apply pressure and force weak returns.
- "Simple, Proven Dink Patterns to Win More Pickleball Points" (The Dink, 2026-05-11)
  https://www.thedinkpickleball.com/simple-proven-dink-patterns-to-win-more-pickleball-points/
  Excerpt: Most pickleball players default to the same cross-court dink pattern over and over, which actually hurts their game. Master these three dink patterns instead to force more errors and win more points.
- "Pickleball Etiquette for Beginners: The Unwritten Rules That Matter" (The Dink, 2026-05-11)
  https://www.thedinkpickleball.com/pickleball-etiquette-for-beginners-unwritten-rules/
  Excerpt: Pickleball etiquette for beginners goes beyond the rulebook, the unwritten social code of the court matters just as much as your backhand. Here's what every new player needs to know before stepping on...
- "Pickleball Strategy Evolution: What's Changed in 9 Years of Tournament Play" (The Dink, 2026-05-11)
  https://www.thedinkpickleball.com/pickleball-strategy-how-the-game-changed-in-9-years/
  Excerpt: Jordan Briones breaks down his gold medal match from 9 years ago, revealing how pickleball strategy has fundamentally shifted. From dinking dominance to aggressive drives, the evolution of pickleball...
- "The Complete Guide for a Better Backhand in Pickleball" (The Dink, 2026-05-11)
  https://www.thedinkpickleball.com/the-complete-guide-for-a-better-backhand-in-pickleball/
  Excerpt: Your backhand technique doesn't have to be a liability. Universal Rackets breaks down the exact mechanics, drills, and mindset shifts that transform your backhand from your weakest shot into your most...

## HISTORY POINTERS (selected milestones from history-seed.json)
- 1965: Pickleball invented on Bainbridge Island — Joel Pritchard, Bill Bell, and Barney McCallum improvise a backyard game at Pritchard's summer cabin at Pleasant Beach on Bainbridge Island, WA, using a badmint
- 1965: Name origin — pickle-boat (official) vs. dog 'Pickles' (folk variant) — USA Pickleball's official position cites Joan Pritchard's pickle-boat metaphor (leftover crew-racing rowers). A widely circulated folk variant credits the Pritc
- 1967: First permanent pickleball court — First known permanent pickleball court built in the backyard of Joel Pritchard's neighbor Bob O'Brian.
- 1972: Pickle Ball, Inc. incorporated — Pickle Ball, Inc. incorporated to protect the name and commercialize equipment.
- 1976: First known pickleball tournament — South Center Athletic Club hosts what is widely considered the first pickleball tournament in Tukwila, WA.
- 1984: USAPA founded; first formal rulebook — The United States Amateur Pickleball Association (USAPA) is founded in Tacoma, WA. The first formal rulebook is published in March 1984. The first National Doub
- 1990: Pickleball played in all 50 US states — Per USAP narrative, by 1990 pickleball is being played in all 50 US states.
- 2005: USAPA rebrands to USA Pickleball Association — The United States Amateur Pickleball Association rebrands to USA Pickleball Association.

---
END OF CONTEXT BUNDLE. If a question cannot be answered from the above, say so honestly.
